agent_output: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC. The architecture uses a region-sovereign data plane pattern: each region owns its customer and operational data with strict residency enforcement, while a lightweight global control plane aggregates anonymized/operational metrics for the global dashboard. Cross-region courier assignment is prevented at the domain level via region-bound aggregate invariants. Resilience is achieved through regional self-sufficiency — each region can operate independently in degraded mode. A CQRS-based global read model fed by domain events enables the real-time operations dashboard without violating data residency.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Regional Data Plane (per region)","responsibility":"Owns all customer PII, order data, courier assignments, and delivery operations for that region. Runs the full application stack: API gateway, application services, domain logic, and regional database. Enforces data residency — no PII leaves the region boundary.","boundaries":"Hard boundary at the network/infrastructure level. Each region is a fully independent deployment (EU-west, US-east, APAC-southeast). No cross-region database replication of PII. Region is encoded in every aggregate root."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Routes customer requests to their home region based on account metadata. Enforces latency SLO (<100ms) by serving from the nearest regional deployment. Handles authentication and rate limiting regionally.","boundaries":"Sits at the edge of each regional deployment. Global DNS (GeoDNS or latency-based routing) directs users to the correct gateway. Account-region binding is authoritative — not just geo-IP."},{"name":"Global Control Plane","responsibility":"Aggregates anonymized operational metrics (delivery counts, SLA compliance, courier utilization, regional health) from all regions. Serves the global operations dashboard. Does NOT store or transit any customer PII. Manages global configuration: region definitions, feature flags, cross-region routing rules.","boundaries":"Deployed in a designated primary region (EU, for GDPR-safe default) with read replicas. Receives only pre-aggregated, anonymized event streams from regional data planes. Has no write path back to regional data."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard","responsibility":"Real-time unified view of logistics metrics across all three regions. Connects to the Global Control Plane read model. Shows per-region and aggregate KPIs.","boundaries":"Read-only consumer of the global control plane. Cannot query regional databases directly. All data it receives is already anonymized/aggregated."},{"name":"Event Bus (per region + global bridge)","responsibility":"Regional event bus handles intra-region domain events (order placed, courier assigned, delivery completed). Global event bridge forwards anonymized/operational event summaries to the global control plane. Filtering at the bridge ensures no PII crosses region boundaries.","boundaries":"Each region has its own event bus (e.g., regional Kafka/EventBridge). The global bridge is a separate component with explicit allowlist of event types and a PII-stripping transformation layer."},{"name":"Regional Failover Controller","responsibility":"Monitors regional health and orchestrates degraded-mode operation. When a region's primary systems fail, activates read-from-cache and queue-writes patterns to maintain functional (degraded) service.","boundaries":"Operates within each region. Does NOT failover to another region (that would violate data residency). Instead activates local redundancy: multi-AZ failover, cached read paths, and durable write queues."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Customer Client","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"HTTPS REST/gRPC. GeoDNS routes to nearest region. Auth token contains region claim. Gateway validates region match.","coupling_type":"loose — client is region-unaware, DNS handles routing"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Data Plane","contract":"Internal service mesh calls. All requests scoped to the region's bounded context. No cross-region fan-out for customer operations.","coupling_type":"tight within region, zero coupling across regions"},{"from":"Regional Data Plane","to":"Regional Event Bus","contract":"Domain events published on order lifecycle, courier assignment, delivery status changes. Events carry region identifier.","coupling_type":"async, loose — publishers and consumers decoupled via event bus"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Global Event Bridge","contract":"Allowlisted event types only. PII-stripping transformation applied before forwarding. Schema: {eventType, regionId, timestamp, aggregatedMetrics}. No customer names, addresses, or identifiers cross the bridge.","coupling_type":"async, very loose — explicit transformation boundary acts as ACL"},{"from":"Global Event Bridge","to":"Global Control Plane","contract":"Anonymized operational events ingested into time-series/OLAP store. Dashboard queries via read-optimized API.","coupling_type":"async, loose — eventual consistency (seconds, not minutes)"},{"from":"Global Operations Dashboard","to":"Global Control Plane","contract":"Read-only query API (GraphQL or REST). Supports real-time subscriptions via WebSocket for live metric updates.","coupling_type":"loose — read-only consumer"}],"data_flow":"1. Customer request → GeoDNS → Regional API Gateway (in-region, <100ms). 2. Gateway authenticates, validates region claim, forwards to regional application services. 3. Domain logic executes within regional data plane; all reads/writes hit regional database only. 4. Domain events published to regional event bus. 5. Regional consumers process events (notifications, courier dispatch, etc.) within region. 6. Global Event Bridge subscribes to regional event bus, strips PII, forwards anonymized summaries to Global Control Plane. 7. Global Control Plane materializes real-time dashboard views. 8. Operations team queries Global Dashboard, which reads from Control Plane.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Region-Sovereign Data Plane","rationale":"Each region is a fully independent deployment owning its data. This is the only pattern that can guarantee data residency compliance (GDPR, CCPA) at the infrastructure level rather than relying on application-level controls that can be misconfigured."},{"pattern":"CQRS with Global Read Model","rationale":"The global dashboard needs cross-region visibility without violating data residency. CQRS separates the command path (regional, sovereign) from the query path (global, anonymized). The global read model is eventually consistent but real-time enough for operational dashboards."},{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (PII-Stripping Event Bridge)","rationale":"The global event bridge acts as an ACL between regional bounded contexts and the global control plane. It transforms domain events into anonymized operational events, preventing PII leakage across region boundaries. This is the critical compliance enforcement point."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead (Regional Isolation)","rationale":"Each region is a bulkhead. EU going down does not affect US or APAC. Within a region, multi-AZ deployment provides the degraded-mode resilience requirement."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture","rationale":"Domain events decouple regional components and enable the global aggregation pattern without synchronous cross-region calls, which would destroy the <100ms latency SLO."},{"pattern":"Cell-Based Architecture","rationale":"Each region operates as an independent cell with its own full stack. This is the industry-proven pattern for multi-region systems with data sovereignty requirements (used by AWS, Slack, etc.). Cells share nothing except anonymized event streams."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management (per region)","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: orderId, contains lineItems, deliveryAddress, regionId — invariant: regionId is immutable after creation)","DeliveryAssignment (root: assignmentId, links orderId to courierId — invariant: courier.regionId must equal order.regionId)"]},{"name":"Courier Management (per region)","classification":"core","aggregates":["Courier (root: courierId, contains availability, location, regionId — invariant: regionId is immutable, courier can only be assigned deliveries in their region)","CourierShift (root: shiftId, contains schedule, zone — scoped to region)"]},{"name":"Customer Identity (per region)","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer (root: customerId, contains PII: name, email, address, regionId — invariant: regionId determines data residency, cannot be changed without data migration process)"]},{"name":"Global Operations (single, cross-region)","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionalHealthSnapshot (root: regionId + timestamp, contains aggregated metrics: orderCount, avgDeliveryTime, courierUtilization, slaCompliance — NO PII)","OperationalAlert (root: alertId, contains regionId, severity, metric thresholds)"]},{"name":"Billing & Payments (per region)","classification":"generic","aggregates":["Invoice (root: invoiceId, regionId — financial data residency aligns with customer data residency)"]},{"name":"Notifications (per region)","classification":"generic","aggregates":["NotificationTemplate (root: templateId)","NotificationRecord (root: notificationId, contains delivery channel, status — PII handled in-region only)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Courier Management","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Management publishes DeliveryRequested events; Courier Management consumes them to assign couriers. Both contexts enforce the region invariant independently (defense in depth)."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Global Operations","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer. Regional Order events are transformed by the PII-stripping Event Bridge into anonymized operational summaries before reaching Global Operations. Global Operations conforms to the anonymized schema, never the full domain model."},{"upstream":"Courier Management","downstream":"Global Operations","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer. Same pattern as Order → Global Operations. Courier utilization metrics are anonymized before crossing the region boundary."},{"upstream":"Customer Identity","downstream":"Order Management","relationship":"Conformist. Order Management uses the Customer context's identity and address model as-is. Both are co-located in the same region, so no data residency concern for this relationship."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Billing & Payments","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order completion events trigger invoice generation. Both in-region."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully independent regional deployments (cell architecture) rather than a single global deployment with data partitioning","gains":"Guaranteed data residency at infrastructure level (not just application logic). Complete blast radius isolation — one region's failure cannot cascade. Independent scaling per region. Simplifies compliance audits — auditors can inspect one region in isolation.","sacrifices":"Higher operational overhead — three full deployments to manage. Code deployment must be coordinated across regions. No shared database means no simple cross-region queries. Customer migration between regions is a complex data-portability operation.","alternatives_considered":["Single global deployment with row-level data partitioning — rejected because data residency enforcement depends on application logic correctness, which is insufficient for GDPR/CCPA compliance","Active-active multi-master database with region-aware replication filters — rejected because replication filter misconfiguration could leak PII across regions, and the operational complexity of multi-master conflict resolution is not justified"]},{"decision":"Anonymized event bridge for global dashboard rather than direct cross-region database queries","gains":"Zero PII crosses region boundaries by design. The transformation layer is auditable and testable. Dashboard latency is acceptable (seconds of eventual consistency). Decouples dashboard availability from regional data plane availability.","sacrifices":"Dashboard shows slightly stale data (seconds, not real-time in the strictest sense). Cannot drill down to individual customer/order details from the global view — operators must switch to the regional dashboard for that. Adds an additional component (event bridge) to operate.","alternatives_considered":["Federated GraphQL querying regional databases directly — rejected because query routing could accidentally pull PII across regions, and a failed region would break the global dashboard","Periodic ETL of anonymized data — rejected because it cannot meet the real-time dashboard requirement"]},{"decision":"In-region degraded mode rather than cross-region failover for resilience","gains":"Data residency is maintained even during outages — EU data never fails over to US. Degraded service (cached reads, queued writes) is better than no service. Avoids the immense complexity of cross-region state synchronization.","sacrifices":"During regional degradation, some operations are unavailable (e.g., new customer registration may be queued). Recovery depends on regional infrastructure — no geographic failover escape hatch. RTO is bounded by regional recovery, not by instant failover.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region failover with encrypted data migration — rejected because even temporary PII presence in another region may violate GDPR data residency requirements, and the legal/compliance risk outweighs the availability gain"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., Middle East, Africa)","evolution_path":"The cell-based architecture makes adding a region a deployment exercise, not an architectural change. Deploy the regional stack, configure GeoDNS, connect the event bridge to the global control plane. The domain model already parameterizes on regionId."},{"area":"Evolving compliance requirements","evolution_path":"Each region's data plane can have region-specific compliance rules (e.g., APAC may need different retention policies). The regional isolation means compliance changes in one region don't affect others."},{"area":"Global dashboard capabilities","evolution_path":"The anonymized event stream to the global control plane can be enriched with new metric types without changing regional data planes — just add new event transformations to the bridge."},{"area":"Courier cross-training / temporary regional assignment","evolution_path":"If the business later needs couriers to operate across regions temporarily, this can be modeled as a new CourierRegionalLoan aggregate with explicit data-sharing consent, without changing the core region invariant."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Customer regionId is immutable after creation","reason":"Data residency is a legal requirement, not a feature toggle. Changing a customer's region requires a formal data portability process (export from source region, import to target, delete from source). This rigidity is intentional and legally mandated."},{"area":"PII never crosses the event bridge","reason":"The PII-stripping transformation in the event bridge is a hard security boundary. It must be enforced at the infrastructure level (schema validation, allowlists) not just application logic. This is non-negotiable for compliance."},{"area":"Courier-region binding","reason":"A courier's region determines their legal employment context, insurance coverage, and regulatory compliance. Cross-region assignment is not just a data issue — it's a legal/operational constraint. The domain model must enforce this rigidly."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Event bridge PII leakage — a misconfigured transformation could forward PII to the global control plane","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Schema validation at the bridge: allowlist of fields that can cross. Automated compliance tests that scan global control plane data for PII patterns. Regular audit of event bridge transformation rules. Infrastructure-level network policies preventing direct regional DB access from global components."},{"risk":"GeoDNS misrouting — a customer routed to the wrong region would get errors or create data in the wrong region","severity":"high","mitigation":"Account-region binding is authoritative (stored in a lightweight global account registry with ONLY accountId → regionId mapping, no PII). GeoDNS is a performance optimization, not the source of truth. The regional API gateway validates the region claim in the auth token and rejects misrouted requests."},{"risk":"Global dashboard eventual consistency — during a regional event bus backlog, the dashboard may show stale data","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Dashboard displays per-region data freshness indicators (last-event-received timestamp). Alerting on event lag > threshold. Operators understand the data is eventually consistent and can drill into regional dashboards for real-time regional data."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing three independent deployments","severity":"high","mitigation":"Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional deployments. Shared CI/CD pipeline that deploys to all regions with canary/progressive rollout. Centralized observability (the global control plane serves double duty). Runbooks for region-specific operations."},{"risk":"Regional degraded mode may not meet customer expectations — queued writes feel like an outage","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Clear UX for degraded mode: customers can view orders and track deliveries (cached reads) but new order placement shows 'processing' status. SLA communication. Multi-AZ deployment within each region minimizes the frequency of degraded mode activation."},{"risk":"Clock skew and event ordering across regions affecting global dashboard accuracy","severity":"low","mitigation":"Events carry region-local timestamps and global logical clocks. Global control plane uses event timestamps for ordering, not ingestion time. NTP synchronization within regions. Dashboard aggregations are time-windowed (e.g., 1-minute buckets) which absorbs minor skew."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout: (1) Extract the current EU monolith into the cell-based regional architecture with explicit regionId on all aggregates and the event bus infrastructure. (2) Deploy the PII-stripping event bridge and global control plane, validating that no PII crosses the boundary. (3) Deploy the US regional cell, migrate or onboard US customers. (4) Deploy APAC. Each phase is independently valuable and testable. Start with the event bridge and global dashboard — they can be built against the existing EU deployment before any new regions exist, which de-risks the hardest compliance component early.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/regions/ — Terraform/Pulumi modules parameterized by region (eu-west, us-east, apac-southeast)","services/api-gateway/ — regional gateway with region-claim validation middleware","services/order-management/ — add regionId to Order aggregate root, enforce immutability invariant","services/courier-management/ — add regionId to Courier aggregate, enforce region-match invariant on DeliveryAssignment","services/event-bridge/ — new service: subscribes to regional event buses, applies PII-stripping transformations, publishes to global control plane","services/event-bridge/transformations/ — per-event-type transformation rules with field allowlists","services/global-control-plane/ — new service: ingests anonymized events, materializes dashboard read models","services/global-control-plane/api/ — dashboard query API with real-time subscription support","shared/domain/region.ts — Region value object (enum: EU, US, APAC) used across all regional services","shared/domain/events/ — domain event schemas with explicit regionId field and PII classification annotations","tests/compliance/ — automated tests that scan global control plane data stores for PII patterns","tests/compliance/event-bridge-pii-filter.test.ts — property-based tests ensuring no PII field survives transformation","docs/adr/001-cell-based-multi-region.md — Architecture Decision Record for the cell-based approach","docs/adr/002-pii-stripping-event-bridge.md — ADR for the global dashboard data flow","docs/runbooks/regional-degraded-mode.md — operational runbook for degraded mode activation/recovery"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture for regional isolation. CQRS for global read model. Anti-Corruption Layer at the event bridge. Domain events for all cross-component communication. Value Objects for Region (not raw strings). Aggregate invariants enforced in domain logic, not just database constraints. Infrastructure-as-Code with region parameterization. Schema-first event contracts with PII field annotations.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT use a shared database across regions — even with encryption, co-located PII from different jurisdictions creates compliance risk","Do NOT rely on application-level logic alone for data residency — enforce at infrastructure level (network policies, separate database instances)","Do NOT allow the global dashboard to have write access to any regional system","Do NOT use geo-IP as the sole mechanism for region routing — it is unreliable and legally insufficient. Use account-region binding as the authority","Do NOT replicate customer PII to a global identity provider — use a minimal global account registry (accountId → regionId only)","Do NOT skip the PII-stripping transformation tests — a single leaked field could trigger a GDPR violation","Do NOT implement cross-region failover for customer data — degraded in-region service is the legally correct choice"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement via cell-based architecture — this is the industry-standard approach and the only one that satisfies GDPR/CCPA at the infrastructure level","PII-stripping event bridge for global dashboard — well-established pattern for cross-boundary data aggregation","Regional courier binding as a domain invariant — directly maps to the business rule and legal constraints","In-region degraded mode over cross-region failover — legally mandated by data residency requirements"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency budget for event bridge to global dashboard — 'real-time' for an operations dashboard likely means seconds, but the specific SLO should be confirmed with the operations team","APAC-specific compliance requirements — GDPR and CCPA are addressed, but APAC may have additional data residency laws (e.g., China's PIPL, India's DPDP Act) that could require further architectural constraints","Degraded mode UX specifics — which operations are available in degraded mode depends on business priority decisions that are outside pure architecture"],"assumptions":["The existing EU deployment is a monolithic or modular application that can be refactored to add regionId to its domain model","The global operations dashboard requires near-real-time data (seconds of lag acceptable) rather than strict real-time (sub-second)","Customer accounts are bound to a single region — there is no requirement for a single customer to have data in multiple regions simultaneously","The platform uses cloud infrastructure that supports multi-region deployment (AWS, GCP, or Azure)","Courier employment and regulatory compliance is strictly regional — no cross-border courier operations are needed currently"]}}

The architecture uses a region-sovereign data plane pattern: each region owns its customer and operational data with strict residency enforcement, while a lightweight global control plane aggregates anonymized/operational metrics for the global dashboard. Let me analyze this multi-region logistics platform architecture problem. I have sufficient constraints to proceed.

Classification: `none` — sufficient constraints exist. We have clear NFRs (data residency, latency, availability), scale requirements (3 regions), compliance requirements (GDPR, CCPA), and operational requirements (global dashboard, degraded mode).

Let me design this comprehensively.

Decouples dashboard availability from regional data plane availability.","sacrifices":"Dashboard shows slightly stale data (seconds, not real-time in the strictest sense). Cannot drill down to individual customer/order details from the global view — operators must switch to the regional dashboard for that. Adds an additional component (event bridge) to operate.","alternatives_considered":["Federated GraphQL querying regional databases directly — rejected because query routing could accidentally pull PII across regions, and a failed region would break the global dashboard","Periodic ETL of anonymized data — rejected because it cannot meet the real-time dashboard requirement"]},{"decision":"In-region degraded mode rather than cross-region failover for resilience","gains":"Data residency is maintained even during outages — EU data never fails over to US. Degraded service (cached reads, queued writes) is better than no service. Avoids the immense complexity of cross-region state synchronization.","sacrifices":"During regional degradation, some operations are unavailable (e.g., new customer registration may be queued). Recovery depends on regional infrastructure — no geographic failover escape hatch. RTO is bounded by regional recovery, not by instant failover.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region failover with encrypted data migration — rejected because even temporary PII presence in another region may violate GDPR data residency requirements, and the legal/compliance risk outweighs the availability gain"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., Middle East, Africa)","evolution_path":"The cell-based architecture makes adding a region a deployment exercise, not an architectural change. Deploy the regional stack, configure GeoDNS, connect the event bridge to the global control plane. The domain model already parameterizes on regionId."},{"area":"Evolving compliance requirements","evolution_path":"Each region's data plane can have region-specific compliance rules (e.g., APAC may need different retention policies). The regional isolation means compliance changes in one region don't affect others."},{"area":"Global dashboard capabilities","evolution_path":"The anonymized event stream to the global control plane can be enriched with new metric types without changing regional data planes — just add new event transformations to the bridge."},{"area":"Courier cross-training / temporary regional assignment","evolution_path":"If the business later needs couriers to operate across regions temporarily, this can be modeled as a new CourierRegionalLoan aggregate with explicit data-sharing consent, without changing the core region invariant."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Customer regionId is immutable after creation","reason":"Data residency is a legal requirement, not a feature toggle. Changing a customer's region requires a formal data portability process (export from source region, import to target, delete from source). This rigidity is intentional and legally mandated."},{"area":"PII never crosses the event bridge","reason":"The PII-stripping transformation in the event bridge is a hard security boundary. It must be enforced at the infrastructure level (schema validation, allowlists) not just application logic. This is non-negotiable for compliance."},{"area":"Courier-region binding","reason":"A courier's region determines their legal employment context, insurance coverage, and regulatory compliance. Cross-region assignment is not just a data issue — it's a legal/operational constraint. The domain model must enforce this rigidly."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Event bridge PII leakage — a misconfigured transformation could forward PII to the global control plane","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Schema validation at the bridge: allowlist of fields that can cross. Automated compliance tests that scan global control plane data for PII patterns. Regular audit of event bridge transformation rules. Infrastructure-level network policies preventing direct regional DB access from global components."},{"risk":"GeoDNS misrouting — a customer routed to the wrong region would get errors or create data in the wrong region","severity":"high","mitigation":"Account-region binding is authoritative (stored in a lightweight global account registry with ONLY accountId → regionId mapping, no PII). GeoDNS is a performance optimization, not the source of truth. The regional API gateway validates the region claim in the auth token and rejects misrouted requests."},{"risk":"Global dashboard eventual consistency — during a regional event bus backlog, the dashboard may show stale data","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Dashboard displays per-region data freshness indicators (last-event-received timestamp). Alerting on event lag > threshold. Operators understand the data is eventually consistent and can drill into regional dashboards for real-time regional data."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing three independent deployments","severity":"high","mitigation":"Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional deployments. Shared CI/CD pipeline that deploys to all regions with canary/progressive rollout. Centralized observability (the global control plane serves double duty). Runbooks for region-specific operations."},{"risk":"Regional degraded mode may not meet customer expectations — queued writes feel like an outage","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Clear UX for degraded mode: customers can view orders and track deliveries (cached reads) but new order placement shows 'processing' status. SLA communication. Multi-AZ deployment within each region minimizes the frequency of degraded mode activation."},{"risk":"Clock skew and event ordering across regions affecting global dashboard accuracy","severity":"low","mitigation":"Events carry region-local timestamps and global logical clocks. Global control plane uses event timestamps for ordering, not ingestion time. NTP synchronization within regions. Dashboard aggregations are time-windowed (e.g., 1-minute buckets) which absorbs minor skew."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout: (1) Extract the current EU monolith into the cell-based regional architecture with explicit regionId on all aggregates and the event bus infrastructure. (2) Deploy the PII-stripping event bridge and global control plane, validating that no PII crosses the boundary. (3) Deploy the US regional cell, migrate or onboard US customers. (4) Deploy APAC. Each phase is independently valuable and testable. Start with the event bridge and global dashboard — they can be built against the existing EU deployment before any new regions exist, which de-risks the hardest compliance component early.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/regions/ — Terraform/Pulumi modules parameterized by region (eu-west, us-east, apac-southeast)","services/api-gateway/ — regional gateway with region-claim validation middleware","services/order-management/ — add regionId to Order aggregate root, enforce immutability invariant","services/courier-management/ — add regionId to Courier aggregate, enforce region-match invariant on DeliveryAssignment","services/event-bridge/ — new service: subscribes to regional event buses, applies PII-stripping transformations, publishes to global control plane","services/event-bridge/transformations/ — per-event-type transformation rules with field allowlists","services/global-control-plane/ — new service: ingests anonymized events, materializes dashboard read models","services/global-control-plane/api/ — dashboard query API with real-time subscription support","shared/domain/region.ts — Region value object (enum: EU, US, APAC) used across all regional services","shared/domain/events/ — domain event schemas with explicit regionId field and PII classification annotations","tests/compliance/ — automated tests that scan global control plane data stores for PII patterns","tests/compliance/event-bridge-pii-filter.test.ts — property-based tests ensuring no PII field survives transformation","docs/adr/001-cell-based-multi-region.md — Architecture Decision Record for the cell-based approach","docs/adr/002-pii-stripping-event-bridge.md — ADR for the global dashboard data flow","docs/runbooks/regional-degraded-mode.md — operational runbook for degraded mode activation/recovery"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture for regional isolation. CQRS for global read model. Anti-Corruption Layer at the event bridge. Domain events for all cross-component communication. Value Objects for Region (not raw strings). Aggregate invariants enforced in domain logic, not just database constraints. Infrastructure-as-Code with region parameterization. Schema-first event contracts with PII field annotations.","pitfalls_to_avoid":["Do NOT use a shared database across regions — even with encryption, co-located PII from different jurisdictions creates compliance risk","Do NOT rely on application-level logic alone for data residency — enforce at infrastructure level (network policies, separate database instances)","Do NOT allow the global dashboard to have write access to any regional system","Do NOT use geo-IP as the sole mechanism for region routing — it is unreliable and legally insufficient. Use account-region binding as the authority","Do NOT replicate customer PII to a global identity provider — use a minimal global account registry (accountId → regionId only)","Do NOT skip the PII-stripping transformation tests — a single leaked field could trigger a GDPR violation","Do NOT implement cross-region failover for customer data — degraded in-region service is the legally correct choice"]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":88,"high_confidence_areas":["Data residency enforcement via cell-based architecture — this is the industry-standard approach and the only one that satisfies GDPR/CCPA at the infrastructure level","PII-stripping event bridge for global dashboard — well-established pattern for cross-boundary data aggregation","Regional courier binding as a domain invariant — directly maps to the business rule and legal constraints","In-region degraded mode over cross-region failover — legally mandated by data residency requirements"],"low_confidence_areas":["Exact latency budget for event bridge to global dashboard — 'real-time' for an operations dashboard likely means seconds, but the specific SLO should be confirmed with the operations team","APAC-specific compliance requirements — GDPR and CCPA are addressed, but APAC may have additional data residency laws (e.g., China's PIPL, India's DPDP Act) that could require further architectural constraints","Degraded mode UX specifics — which operations are available in degraded mode depends on business priority decisions that are outside pure architecture"],"assumptions":["The existing EU deployment is a monolithic or modular application that can be refactored to add regionId to its domain model","The global operations dashboard requires near-real-time data (seconds of lag acceptable) rather than strict real-time (sub-second)","Customer accounts are bound to a single region — there is no requirement for a single customer to have data in multiple regions simultaneously","The platform uses cloud infrastructure that supports multi-region deployment (AWS, GCP, or Azure)","Courier employment and regulatory compliance is strictly regional — no cross-border courier operations are needed currently"]}}

trace.result: {"executive_summary":"Multi-region logistics platform expanding from single-region EU to EU + US + APAC. The architecture uses a region-sovereign data plane pattern: each region owns its customer and operational data with strict residency enforcement, while a lightweight global control plane aggregates anonymized/operational metrics for the global dashboard. Cross-region courier assignment is prevented at the domain level via region-bound aggregate invariants. Resilience is achieved through regional self-sufficiency — each region can operate independently in degraded mode. A CQRS-based global read model fed by domain events enables the real-time operations dashboard without violating data residency.","architecture":{"components":[{"name":"Regional Data Plane (per region)","responsibility":"Owns all customer PII, order data, courier assignments, and delivery operations for that region. Runs the full application stack: API gateway, application services, domain logic, and regional database. Enforces data residency — no PII leaves the region boundary.","boundaries":"Hard boundary at the network/infrastructure level. Each region is a fully independent deployment (EU-west, US-east, APAC-southeast). No cross-region database replication of PII. Region is encoded in every aggregate root."},{"name":"Regional API Gateway","responsibility":"Routes customer requests to their home region based on account metadata. Enforces latency SLO (<100ms) by serving from the nearest regional deployment. Handles authentication and rate limiting regionally.","boundaries":"Sits at the edge of each regional deployment. Global DNS (GeoDNS or latency-based routing) directs users to the correct gateway. Account-region binding is authoritative — not just geo-IP."},{"name":"Global Control Plane","responsibility":"Aggregates anonymized operational metrics (delivery counts, SLA compliance, courier utilization, regional health) from all regions. Serves the global operations dashboard. Does NOT store or transit any customer PII. Manages global configuration: region definitions, feature flags, cross-region routing rules.","boundaries":"Deployed in a designated primary region (EU, for GDPR-safe default) with read replicas. Receives only pre-aggregated, anonymized event streams from regional data planes. Has no write path back to regional data."},{"name":"Global Operations Dashboard","responsibility":"Real-time unified view of logistics metrics across all three regions. Connects to the Global Control Plane read model. Shows per-region and aggregate KPIs.","boundaries":"Read-only consumer of the global control plane. Cannot query regional databases directly. All data it receives is already anonymized/aggregated."},{"name":"Event Bus (per region + global bridge)","responsibility":"Regional event bus handles intra-region domain events (order placed, courier assigned, delivery completed). Global event bridge forwards anonymized/operational event summaries to the global control plane. Filtering at the bridge ensures no PII crosses region boundaries.","boundaries":"Each region has its own event bus (e.g., regional Kafka/EventBridge). The global bridge is a separate component with explicit allowlist of event types and a PII-stripping transformation layer."},{"name":"Regional Failover Controller","responsibility":"Monitors regional health and orchestrates degraded-mode operation. When a region's primary systems fail, activates read-from-cache and queue-writes patterns to maintain functional (degraded) service.","boundaries":"Operates within each region. Does NOT failover to another region (that would violate data residency). Instead activates local redundancy: multi-AZ failover, cached read paths, and durable write queues."}],"interfaces":[{"from":"Customer Client","to":"Regional API Gateway","contract":"HTTPS REST/gRPC. GeoDNS routes to nearest region. Auth token contains region claim. Gateway validates region match.","coupling_type":"loose — client is region-unaware, DNS handles routing"},{"from":"Regional API Gateway","to":"Regional Data Plane","contract":"Internal service mesh calls. All requests scoped to the region's bounded context. No cross-region fan-out for customer operations.","coupling_type":"tight within region, zero coupling across regions"},{"from":"Regional Data Plane","to":"Regional Event Bus","contract":"Domain events published on order lifecycle, courier assignment, delivery status changes. Events carry region identifier.","coupling_type":"async, loose — publishers and consumers decoupled via event bus"},{"from":"Regional Event Bus","to":"Global Event Bridge","contract":"Allowlisted event types only. PII-stripping transformation applied before forwarding. Schema: {eventType, regionId, timestamp, aggregatedMetrics}. No customer names, addresses, or identifiers cross the bridge.","coupling_type":"async, very loose — explicit transformation boundary acts as ACL"},{"from":"Global Event Bridge","to":"Global Control Plane","contract":"Anonymized operational events ingested into time-series/OLAP store. Dashboard queries via read-optimized API.","coupling_type":"async, loose — eventual consistency (seconds, not minutes)"},{"from":"Global Operations Dashboard","to":"Global Control Plane","contract":"Read-only query API (GraphQL or REST). Supports real-time subscriptions via WebSocket for live metric updates.","coupling_type":"loose — read-only consumer"}],"data_flow":"1. Customer request → GeoDNS → Regional API Gateway (in-region, <100ms). 2. Gateway authenticates, validates region claim, forwards to regional application services. 3. Domain logic executes within regional data plane; all reads/writes hit regional database only. 4. Domain events published to regional event bus. 5. Regional consumers process events (notifications, courier dispatch, etc.) within region. 6. Global Event Bridge subscribes to regional event bus, strips PII, forwards anonymized summaries to Global Control Plane. 7. Global Control Plane materializes real-time dashboard views. 8. Operations team queries Global Dashboard, which reads from Control Plane.","patterns_used":[{"pattern":"Region-Sovereign Data Plane","rationale":"Each region is a fully independent deployment owning its data. This is the only pattern that can guarantee data residency compliance (GDPR, CCPA) at the infrastructure level rather than relying on application-level controls that can be misconfigured."},{"pattern":"CQRS with Global Read Model","rationale":"The global dashboard needs cross-region visibility without violating data residency. CQRS separates the command path (regional, sovereign) from the query path (global, anonymized). The global read model is eventually consistent but real-time enough for operational dashboards."},{"pattern":"Anti-Corruption Layer (PII-Stripping Event Bridge)","rationale":"The global event bridge acts as an ACL between regional bounded contexts and the global control plane. It transforms domain events into anonymized operational events, preventing PII leakage across region boundaries. This is the critical compliance enforcement point."},{"pattern":"Bulkhead (Regional Isolation)","rationale":"Each region is a bulkhead. EU going down does not affect US or APAC. Within a region, multi-AZ deployment provides the degraded-mode resilience requirement."},{"pattern":"Event-Driven Architecture","rationale":"Domain events decouple regional components and enable the global aggregation pattern without synchronous cross-region calls, which would destroy the <100ms latency SLO."},{"pattern":"Cell-Based Architecture","rationale":"Each region operates as an independent cell with its own full stack. This is the industry-proven pattern for multi-region systems with data sovereignty requirements (used by AWS, Slack, etc.). Cells share nothing except anonymized event streams."}]},"domain_model":{"bounded_contexts":[{"name":"Order Management (per region)","classification":"core","aggregates":["Order (root: orderId, contains lineItems, deliveryAddress, regionId — invariant: regionId is immutable after creation)","DeliveryAssignment (root: assignmentId, links orderId to courierId — invariant: courier.regionId must equal order.regionId)"]},{"name":"Courier Management (per region)","classification":"core","aggregates":["Courier (root: courierId, contains availability, location, regionId — invariant: regionId is immutable, courier can only be assigned deliveries in their region)","CourierShift (root: shiftId, contains schedule, zone — scoped to region)"]},{"name":"Customer Identity (per region)","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["Customer (root: customerId, contains PII: name, email, address, regionId — invariant: regionId determines data residency, cannot be changed without data migration process)"]},{"name":"Global Operations (single, cross-region)","classification":"supporting","aggregates":["RegionalHealthSnapshot (root: regionId + timestamp, contains aggregated metrics: orderCount, avgDeliveryTime, courierUtilization, slaCompliance — NO PII)","OperationalAlert (root: alertId, contains regionId, severity, metric thresholds)"]},{"name":"Billing & Payments (per region)","classification":"generic","aggregates":["Invoice (root: invoiceId, regionId — financial data residency aligns with customer data residency)"]},{"name":"Notifications (per region)","classification":"generic","aggregates":["NotificationTemplate (root: templateId)","NotificationRecord (root: notificationId, contains delivery channel, status — PII handled in-region only)"]}],"context_map":[{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Courier Management","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order Management publishes DeliveryRequested events; Courier Management consumes them to assign couriers. Both contexts enforce the region invariant independently (defense in depth)."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Global Operations","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer. Regional Order events are transformed by the PII-stripping Event Bridge into anonymized operational summaries before reaching Global Operations. Global Operations conforms to the anonymized schema, never the full domain model."},{"upstream":"Courier Management","downstream":"Global Operations","relationship":"Anti-Corruption Layer. Same pattern as Order → Global Operations. Courier utilization metrics are anonymized before crossing the region boundary."},{"upstream":"Customer Identity","downstream":"Order Management","relationship":"Conformist. Order Management uses the Customer context's identity and address model as-is. Both are co-located in the same region, so no data residency concern for this relationship."},{"upstream":"Order Management","downstream":"Billing & Payments","relationship":"Customer-Supplier. Order completion events trigger invoice generation. Both in-region."}]},"trade_offs":[{"decision":"Fully independent regional deployments (cell architecture) rather than a single global deployment with data partitioning","gains":"Guaranteed data residency at infrastructure level (not just application logic). Complete blast radius isolation — one region's failure cannot cascade. Independent scaling per region. Simplifies compliance audits — auditors can inspect one region in isolation.","sacrifices":"Higher operational overhead — three full deployments to manage. Code deployment must be coordinated across regions. No shared database means no simple cross-region queries. Customer migration between regions is a complex data-portability operation.","alternatives_considered":["Single global deployment with row-level data partitioning — rejected because data residency enforcement depends on application logic correctness, which is insufficient for GDPR/CCPA compliance","Active-active multi-master database with region-aware replication filters — rejected because replication filter misconfiguration could leak PII across regions, and the operational complexity of multi-master conflict resolution is not justified"]},{"decision":"Anonymized event bridge for global dashboard rather than direct cross-region database queries","gains":"Zero PII crosses region boundaries by design. The transformation layer is auditable and testable. Dashboard latency is acceptable (seconds of eventual consistency). Decouples dashboard availability from regional data plane availability.","sacrifices":"Dashboard shows slightly stale data (seconds, not real-time in the strictest sense). Cannot drill down to individual customer/order details from the global view — operators must switch to the regional dashboard for that. Adds an additional component (event bridge) to operate.","alternatives_considered":["Federated GraphQL querying regional databases directly — rejected because query routing could accidentally pull PII across regions, and a failed region would break the global dashboard","Periodic ETL of anonymized data — rejected because it cannot meet the real-time dashboard requirement"]},{"decision":"In-region degraded mode rather than cross-region failover for resilience","gains":"Data residency is maintained even during outages — EU data never fails over to US. Degraded service (cached reads, queued writes) is better than no service. Avoids the immense complexity of cross-region state synchronization.","sacrifices":"During regional degradation, some operations are unavailable (e.g., new customer registration may be queued). Recovery depends on regional infrastructure — no geographic failover escape hatch. RTO is bounded by regional recovery, not by instant failover.","alternatives_considered":["Cross-region failover with encrypted data migration — rejected because even temporary PII presence in another region may violate GDPR data residency requirements, and the legal/compliance risk outweighs the availability gain"]}],"flexibility_points":[{"area":"Adding new regions (e.g., Middle East, Africa)","evolution_path":"The cell-based architecture makes adding a region a deployment exercise, not an architectural change. Deploy the regional stack, configure GeoDNS, connect the event bridge to the global control plane. The domain model already parameterizes on regionId."},{"area":"Evolving compliance requirements","evolution_path":"Each region's data plane can have region-specific compliance rules (e.g., APAC may need different retention policies). The regional isolation means compliance changes in one region don't affect others."},{"area":"Global dashboard capabilities","evolution_path":"The anonymized event stream to the global control plane can be enriched with new metric types without changing regional data planes — just add new event transformations to the bridge."},{"area":"Courier cross-training / temporary regional assignment","evolution_path":"If the business later needs couriers to operate across regions temporarily, this can be modeled as a new CourierRegionalLoan aggregate with explicit data-sharing consent, without changing the core region invariant."}],"rigidity_points":[{"area":"Customer regionId is immutable after creation","reason":"Data residency is a legal requirement, not a feature toggle. Changing a customer's region requires a formal data portability process (export from source region, import to target, delete from source). This rigidity is intentional and legally mandated."},{"area":"PII never crosses the event bridge","reason":"The PII-stripping transformation in the event bridge is a hard security boundary. It must be enforced at the infrastructure level (schema validation, allowlists) not just application logic. This is non-negotiable for compliance."},{"area":"Courier-region binding","reason":"A courier's region determines their legal employment context, insurance coverage, and regulatory compliance. Cross-region assignment is not just a data issue — it's a legal/operational constraint. The domain model must enforce this rigidly."}],"risks":[{"risk":"Event bridge PII leakage — a misconfigured transformation could forward PII to the global control plane","severity":"critical","mitigation":"Schema validation at the bridge: allowlist of fields that can cross. Automated compliance tests that scan global control plane data for PII patterns. Regular audit of event bridge transformation rules. Infrastructure-level network policies preventing direct regional DB access from global components."},{"risk":"GeoDNS misrouting — a customer routed to the wrong region would get errors or create data in the wrong region","severity":"high","mitigation":"Account-region binding is authoritative (stored in a lightweight global account registry with ONLY accountId → regionId mapping, no PII). GeoDNS is a performance optimization, not the source of truth. The regional API gateway validates the region claim in the auth token and rejects misrouted requests."},{"risk":"Global dashboard eventual consistency — during a regional event bus backlog, the dashboard may show stale data","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Dashboard displays per-region data freshness indicators (last-event-received timestamp). Alerting on event lag > threshold. Operators understand the data is eventually consistent and can drill into regional dashboards for real-time regional data."},{"risk":"Operational complexity of managing three independent deployments","severity":"high","mitigation":"Infrastructure-as-Code with parameterized regional deployments. Shared CI/CD pipeline that deploys to all regions with canary/progressive rollout. Centralized observability (the global control plane serves double duty). Runbooks for region-specific operations."},{"risk":"Regional degraded mode may not meet customer expectations — queued writes feel like an outage","severity":"medium","mitigation":"Clear UX for degraded mode: customers can view orders and track deliveries (cached reads) but new order placement shows 'processing' status. SLA communication. Multi-AZ deployment within each region minimizes the frequency of degraded mode activation."},{"risk":"Clock skew and event ordering across regions affecting global dashboard accuracy","severity":"low","mitigation":"Events carry region-local timestamps and global logical clocks. Global control plane uses event timestamps for ordering, not ingestion time. NTP synchronization within regions. Dashboard aggregations are time-windowed (e.g., 1-minute buckets) which absorbs minor skew."}],"implementation_guidance":{"recommended_approach":"Phase the rollout: (1) Extract the current EU monolith into the cell-based regional architecture with explicit regionId on all aggregates and the event bus infrastructure. (2) Deploy the PII-stripping event bridge and global control plane, validating that no PII crosses the boundary. (3) Deploy the US regional cell, migrate or onboard US customers. (4) Deploy APAC. Each phase is independently valuable and testable. Start with the event bridge and global dashboard — they can be built against the existing EU deployment before any new regions exist, which de-risks the hardest compliance component early.","files_to_create_or_modify":["infra/regions/ — Terraform/Pulumi modules parameterized by region (eu-west, us-east, apac-southeast)","services/api-gateway/ — regional gateway with region-claim validation middleware","services/order-management/ — add regionId to Order aggregate root, enforce immutability invariant","services/courier-management/ — add regionId to Courier aggregate, enforce region-match invariant on DeliveryAssignment","services/event-bridge/ — new service: subscribes to regional event buses, applies PII-stripping transformations, publishes to global control plane","services/event-bridge/transformations/ — per-event-type transformation rules with field allowlists","services/global-control-plane/ — new service: ingests anonymized events, materializes dashboard read models","services/global-control-plane/api/ — dashboard query API with real-time subscription support","shared/domain/region.ts — Region value object (enum: EU, US, APAC) used across all regional services","shared/domain/events/ — domain event schemas with explicit regionId field and PII classification annotations","tests/compliance/ — automated tests that scan global control plane data stores for PII patterns","tests/compliance/event-bridge-pii-filter.test.ts — property-based tests ensuring no PII field survives transformation","docs/adr/001-cell-based-multi-region.md — Architecture Decision Record for the cell-based approach","docs/adr/002-pii-stripping-event-bridge.md — ADR for the global dashboard data flow","docs/runbooks/regional-degraded-mode.md — operational runbook for degraded mode activation/recovery"],"patterns_to_follow":"Cell-based architecture for regional isolation.
